title: Phyllorkis weddellii
taxonomy: Phyllorkis weddellii is classified within the family Orchidaceae, which comprises a diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as orchids. This species belongs to the subfamily Orchidoideae and is further categorized under the tribe Coelogyneae. Within this tribe, Phyllorkis weddellii is a part of the genus Phyllorkis, which is closely related to the genus Coelogyne. The genus Phyllorkis is characterized by its unique floral and vegetative features. Phyllorkis weddellii's classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other orchid species.

intro: Phyllorkis weddellii, also known as Weddell's orchid or Kalmegh, is a plant species belonging to the family Gentianaceae. The plant is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which can help in reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. Phyllorkis weddellii contains bioactive constituents such as wedelolactone, wedelol, and other flavonoids and alkaloids that contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly prepared as an herbal tea, extract, or powder for use in traditional remedies, and is also used in some modern supplements for its potential health benefits.
